title: The Whale from West Blue
description: On pages 18–20, the whale from west blue as inside the whale, the crew meets Crocus and begins to understand Laboon’s pain and strange living arrangements.
scene: The Whale from West Blue. Inside the whale, the crew meets Crocus and begins to understand Laboon’s pain and strange living arrangements. The scene focuses on the immediate character conflict rather than background exposition.

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: This whale is an island whale, found normally in West Blue.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: It's the largest species of whale in the world. This one's name is Laboon.

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: And these two are Rogers from a nearby town...

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Their goal is to hunt Laboon,

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Because the meat from Laboon can feed their town for 2-3 years.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: But I won't allow that!

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: As for why Laboon keeps on bashing his head against the red line,

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: And bellows while facing reverse mountain... there's a reason for all that too.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Long ago, while I was minding my business as a lighthouse keeper,

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: A certain group of friendly pirates came down reverse mountain.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: and following their ship was a little baby whale. That whale was Laboon.

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: It seems that Laboon had accompanied their journey in West Blue,

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: But fearing that Grand Line was too dangerous, they left him behind or so they thought.

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: Normally, Island whales are known to swim together in pods,

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: But to Laboon, those pirates were his pod members.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Their ship was damaged when they arrived so they stayed here for several months to fix it.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I became quite good friends with them during their stay.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: And so, on the day of their departure, their captain asked me,

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: "Could you please take care of Laboon for 2-3 years?"

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: "Once we've sailed around the world, we'll come back for him no matter what."

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: Under- standing their intent, Laboon patiently waited for them here.

dialogue.speaker: Laboon
dialogue.text: BUOO Hhh...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: So that's why he keeps on bellowing even today... and bashing himself against red line...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: That's right...

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: It's been over 50 years since then...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: He still believes his friends will come back...

summary: This part advances the chapter by focusing on the whale from west blue. It keeps the main tension clear while preserving the dialogue that drives the scene.
